spacecraft.description: Local execution, maneuverability, state quality, and disposal constraints.

neighborhood.description: Adjacent satellites, shared shells, cross-operator interactions, and maneuver intent.

infrastructure.description: Fleet routing, load balancing, service continuity, and common-mode failures.

operators.operator: US Army Space and Missile Defense Command
DGA (Arms Procurement Agency)/Centre National d'Etudes Spatiales (CNES)
Indonesian National Aeronautics and  Space Agency (Lembaga Penerbangan dan Antariksa Nasional - LAPAN)

scenarios.name: Mass conjunction alert
scenarios.description: Many satellites in dense low-altitude shells receive simultaneous screening alerts.

scenarios.name: Ground station outage
scenarios.description: Traffic must reroute through fewer gateway regions, increasing load-balancing pressure.

scenarios.name: Degraded tracking accuracy
scenarios.description: Uncertainty grows when state estimates or ephemerides are stale.

scenarios.name: Failed software update
scenarios.description: A common-mode autonomy update changes routing or maneuver thresholds across a fleet.

scenarios.name: Cross-operator maneuver conflict
scenarios.description: Two maneuverable active satellites respond to the same event with incompatible assumptions.

scenarios.name: Partial deorbit failure
scenarios.description: A spacecraft loses enough capability that disposal becomes a coordination problem.

messages.audit_relevance: preserves decision evidence without sharing proprietary autonomy code
preserves decision evidence without sharing proprietary autonomy code
preserves decision evidence without sharing proprietary autonomy code
preserves decision evidence without sharing proprietary autonomy code
preserves decision evidence without sharing proprietary autonomy code

evidence_reports.what_failed: Many satellites in dense low-altitude shells receive simultaneous screening alerts.
evidence_reports.what_recovered: Residual risk remains high and requires escalation.
evidence_reports.regulator_summary: Mass conjunction alert affects 692 satellites and requires approximately 3406 SAIL coordination messages.

evidence_reports.what_failed: Traffic must reroute through fewer gateway regions, increasing load-balancing pressure.
evidence_reports.what_recovered: SAIL intent sharing and audit records reduce coordination ambiguity.
evidence_reports.regulator_summary: Ground station outage affects 716 satellites and requires approximately 2566 SAIL coordination messages.

evidence_reports.what_failed: Uncertainty grows when state estimates or ephemerides are stale.
evidence_reports.what_recovered: SAIL intent sharing and audit records reduce coordination ambiguity.
evidence_reports.regulator_summary: Degraded tracking accuracy affects 12 satellites and requires approximately 38 SAIL coordination messages.

evidence_reports.what_failed: A common-mode autonomy update changes routing or maneuver thresholds across a fleet.
evidence_reports.what_recovered: SAIL intent sharing and audit records reduce coordination ambiguity.
evidence_reports.regulator_summary: Failed software update affects 533 satellites and requires approximately 2215 SAIL coordination messages.

evidence_reports.what_failed: Two maneuverable active satellites respond to the same event with incompatible assumptions.
evidence_reports.what_recovered: Residual risk remains high and requires escalation.
evidence_reports.regulator_summary: Cross-operator maneuver conflict affects 464 satellites and requires approximately 2320 SAIL coordination messages.

evidence_reports.what_failed: A spacecraft loses enough capability that disposal becomes a coordination problem.
evidence_reports.what_recovered: SAIL intent sharing and audit records reduce coordination ambiguity.
evidence_reports.regulator_summary: Partial deorbit failure affects 1385 satellites and requires approximately 5535 SAIL coordination messages.
